cards.front: What is the most comprehensive online C++ reference?
cards.back: cppreference.com - comprehensive, accurate, covers all C++ versions

cards.front: What are the two main sections of cppreference.com?
cards.back:
• Language: C++ syntax, keywords, concepts
  • Standard library: STL containers, algorithms, utilities

cards.front: What should you look for when reading C++ reference documentation?
cards.back:
• Syntax and parameters
  • Return type/value
  • Since which C++ version (C++11, C++17, etc.)
  • Example code
  • Related functions

cards.text: When using C++ references, pay attention to {{c1::which C++ standard}} a feature requires (C++11, C++14, C++17, C++20, etc.).

cards.front: Besides cppreference.com, what's another official C++ resource?
cards.back: The C++ standard itself, though it's more technical and harder to read than cppreference.

cards.explanation:
Tips for using C++ references:
  • Start with examples, then read details
  • Check C++ version requirements
  • Look for "Notes" and "Possible implementation" sections
  • Use compiler documentation for extensions
  • Verify with actual code tests
examples: // Try example code to understand behavior

cards.text: The most comprehensive C++ reference is {{c1::cppreference.com}}, covering both {{c2::language}} and {{c3::standard library}}.
